title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 10 September 2024.

items.title: **PS5 Pro** name seemingly confirmed by Sony ahead of imminent reveal
bullets: Sony accidentally uploaded an unlisted video titled PS5 Pro
Technical presentation with Mark Cerny is today at 8am PDT / 11am EDT / 4pm BST

items.title: Bungie announces new **Destiny 2 **release model with two ‘medium’ expansions a year
Disgruntled **Team Fortress 2** players bind 340,000 signatures into a book and hand-deliver it to Valve
bullets: #SaveTF2 was born in 2022, amid a bot situation so severe, it was threatening to make Valve's classic team-based shooter literally unplayable. The community faced daily battle bots using game-ruining hacks, racist usernames, disruptive noises in voice chat, and worse - and with Valve doing little to help, players even took to fighting bots with bots of their own.

items.title: **Apple** says all its new iPhone 16s play AAA games, no iPhone Pro required
bullets: iOS 18 out next week with new Game mode

items.title: **Minecraft** ditching single summer update for more regular releases, mob votes being retired
**Ubisoft** shares plunge again after investor urges company to go private
bullets: Recent releases including Star Wars Outlaws and xDefinant have reportedly missed expectations
Share price fell almost 10% during trading, before closing down 7.13% at €13.67. It has fallen by more than 50% over the last 12 months and now sits close to a ten-year low.

items.title: Embracer studio **Lost Boys Interactive** has made another round of layoffs
bullets: The support studio's credits include Tiny Tina's Wonderlands, Diablo 4 and PUBG
Lost Boys, which also suffered a heavy round of layoffs in January, employs over 200 staff across more than 40 US states and Canada, according to its website.
Unclear at this time how many people are affected

items.title: **PS5 Pro** details expected at Technical Presentation
bullets: Third Party state of play focused on games that benefit from PS5 Pro due September 19th
PS5 Pro supports HDR10+ and Dolby Vision on select streaming services and physical media
PS5 Pro Digital due Nov 14th for £499.99
PS5 Pro "Deluxe" (Disc variant) due December 5th for £589.99
Improved Dualsense with better battery life and enhanced triggers
